fields.description:
## Summary
Cut the 0.10.1 patch release of rebar per docs/releasing.md. This is a fix/patch bump from 0.10.0, covering the ~36 commits landed on main since v0.10.0 (reconciler Backend-port scalar surfaces, plan-review/close-gate hardening, code-health analyzers scc/lizard/jscpd, review-bot token accounting, and assorted fixes).

## Plan
1. Fresh worktree from origin/main (git worktree add / make worktree).
2. Bump versions in lockstep: pyproject.toml [project].version; server.json top-level version AND every packages[].version -> 0.10.1. server.json has exactly ONE packages[] entry, so a correct bump yields two 0.10.1 occurrences in that file. The release authorize job runs release_guards.py version-lockstep and fails the build on any mismatch.
3. CHANGELOG.md: prepend the [0.10.1] section via 'make changelog VERSION=v0.10.1', hand-curate the generated top section, add the [0.10.1] compare-link footer.
4. Local gates, run with the worktree venv on PATH: 'make lint && make typecheck && make test', then build+validate the artifact metadata exactly as the runbook (docs/releasing.md L259) and release.yml specify: 'python -m build && python -m twine check dist/*'. twine is not a dev extra in pyproject; invoke it as the CI lock does via 'pipx run twine check dist/*' (or 'uvx twine check dist/*') if 'python -m twine' is absent in the worktree venv, so the glob-targeted metadata check actually runs against the built wheel+sdist.
5. Commit with 'rebar-ticket: zincoid-panoramic-eeve' + DCO sign-off trailers; push 'git push gerrit HEAD:refs/for/main'; earn LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1; then Submit (POST /a/changes/<n>/submit as RebarBotNava).
6. Fast-forward the worktree to merged main; dispatch the release from main: 'gh workflow run release.yml --ref main -f version=0.10.1' and approve the pypi environment deployment when prompted (required-reviewers gate). PyPI is immutable — if the published artifact is defective, yank it and ship 0.10.2.
7. Verify the channels with the proving commands below.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Version-lockstep holds: 'grep -c 0.10.1 pyproject.toml' >=1 and 'grep -c 0.10.1 server.json' == 2 (top-level + the single packages[] entry) on the release commit; equivalently release_guards.py version-lockstep passes in the authorize job.
- [ ] CHANGELOG.md carries a curated release section: 'grep -E "^## \[0\.10\.1\]" CHANGELOG.md' matches.
- [ ] Release commit landed on main through Gerrit with both gate votes green: the Gerrit change shows LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1 and status MERGED, and 'git log origin/main --oneline | grep -i "release 0.10.1"' finds it.
- [ ] [operator-attested] 0.10.1 published to PyPI: 'curl -s https://pypi.org/pypi/nava-rebar/json | python3 -c "import json,sys;print(json.load(sys.stdin)[\"info\"][\"version\"])"' prints 0.10.1.
- [ ] [operator-attested] v0.10.1 tag + GitHub Release created at the released main SHA: 'gh release view v0.10.1 --json tagName,targetCommitish,isLatest' shows the tag, marked Latest.

## Out of scope / automated follow-ups (no executor action required)
- Homebrew tap self-bump (navapbc/homebrew-rebar autobump.yml, scheduled every 6h) and the MCP-registry publish job (mcp_registry in release.yml, needs: publish) run automatically after publish; verifying them is not a release-blocking acceptance criterion for this ticket.

## Attestation evidence
- The above acceptance criteria are proven in-session by the quoted shell/gh/curl commands whose outputs are recorded as comments on this ticket, plus the Gerrit change number and its +1/+1 vote outcome and MERGED status.
